title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Edith Pettigrew recounts a past incident involving the former maid, Lily Abbot, and Lady Harriet (Helen Kennedy). Lily claimed that Lady Harriet once expressed intense fear of a man, telling him, "I'm afraid of you!" On the night of the disappearance, Lily had gone to the movies with a child named Leonie, leaving the house under the assumption that the master and mistress would return soon. 

After the movies, Dr. Kennedy found Edith in the kitchen and asked Lily to check the mistress's bedroom for missing clothing. Lily returned excited, claiming Lady Harriet had run off with someone, possibly an officer or a chauffeur. However, Lily later became convinced that Lady Harriet had been murdered by the master and buried under the basement floor to stage a disappearance. Lily noted that the missing clothing was inconsistent with a planned elopement; for example, a silver-gray ball gown was taken without its matching belt, bra, and slip, and gold-embroidered shoes were taken without their silver-banded counterparts. Edith had previously dismissed the murder theory because she saw the master walking down a hill with a golf club at the time of the alleged incident, leading her to believe someone else was responsible.

In a separate setting, Joram, using the alias Mr. Reed, visits the Royal Clarence Hotel to manipulate the receptionist, Miss Nelcourt. He claims he is looking for an old guest register to settle a bet with his wife. After Miss Nelcourt provides the register, Joram finds the entry he seeks: Captain Sidney Erskine and his wife stayed at the hotel from July 27 to August 17. Joram returns to a hillside villa and informs Gwendolyn that he believes he has found the person he was looking for.
